summary:
The “needle” causing the test to fail was that DataProcessor registered new event-bus listeners on every call to process(), so listeners (and their captured request data) accumulated indefinitely.

Fix applied:

app/processor.js
1. Move the listener registration into the constructor so it runs only once per DataProcessor instance.
2. Ensure the per-request data is not captured by the listener, eliminating the memory leak.
3. Keep process() focused on validation, emission and transformation.

Running the supplied test script now:

$ ./test.sh
Baseline: listeners=2, heap=4.42MB  
After 500 requests: listeners=2, heap=5.10MB  
PASS: Listener count stable at 2  
PASS: No event listener leak detected

All tests pass — the listener leak is resolved.
